title: address.py: TOFU key mismatch raises ValueError causing service disruption
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/address.py
Lines: 163-170

The AddressMapper.learn() method raises ValueError when receiving a different pubkey for an already-known IID:

```python
if existing_pubkey is not None and existing_pubkey != pubkey:
    raise ValueError(
        f"TOFU violation: pubkey mismatch for IID {iid.hex()} ..."
    )
```

While TOFU key pinning is correct security behavior, raising an exception is the wrong response. A malicious peer could repeatedly send messages with conflicting keys to crash the service.

Impact: Denial of service - attacker sends crafted messages claiming different identities for the same IID, crashing the Meshtastic adapter.

Fix: Log the violation and silently ignore the new key (keeping the pinned one), or return a boolean/enum indicating the outcome instead of raising. The caller should decide how to handle the untrusted message (drop it) without crashing.

```python
if existing_pubkey is not None and existing_pubkey != pubkey:
    log.warning("TOFU violation: pubkey mismatch for IID %s", iid.hex())
    return False  # or raise only if caller explicitly wants strict mode
```
close_reason: Fixed: AddressMapper.learn() now returns bool and logs warning on TOFU violation instead of raising ValueError
